    <h1 {...{
      "id": "package-a-uxp-plugin"
    }}>{`Package a UXP plugin`}</h1>
    <p>{`UXP plugins are packaged as `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.ccx`}</inlineCode>{` files, which can be shared privately or published to the Creative Cloud Marketplace.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "the-ccx-file-format"
    }}>{`The CCX file format`}</h2>
    <p>{`A `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.ccx`}</inlineCode>{` file is a regular ZIP file under the hood. Unless you have some special requirements, we suggest `}<strong parentName="p">{`not to create it manually`}</strong>{` and let the UXP Developer Tool do it for you instead—as we will see later in this guide.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "differences-with-cep-extensions"
    }}>{`Differences with CEP Extensions`}</h3>
    <p>{`Compared to CEP extensions which relied on `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.zxp`}</inlineCode>{` files, the UXP ecosystem simplifies the packaging and distribution process in different ways. Most notably, you `}<strong parentName="p">{`no longer need to apply a Digital Signature and Timestamp the package`}</strong>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`The new `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.ccx`}</inlineCode>{` format is `}<strong parentName="p">{`installed directly by the Creative Cloud Desktop application`}</strong>{`, which has proved to be more reliable than the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ExManCmd`}</inlineCode>{` utility—easier for the final users to install, and less error prone.`}</p>
    <p>{`Finally, UXP doesn't support the use of `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MXI`}</inlineCode>{` files for advanced packaging configuration anymore.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "mind-your-plugins-id"
    }}>{`Mind your Plugin's ID`}</h2>
    <p>{`Before you package your plugin for distribution, make sure that the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` file contains a valid `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`id`}</inlineCode>{` string property. The ID must be a `}<strong parentName="p">{`unique identifier for your plugin`}</strong>{` and it is used to disambiguate plugins when installing them.`}</p>
    <p>{`If you plan to `}<strong parentName="p">{`publish to the Adobe Creative Cloud Marketplace`}</strong>{`, you should obtain the ID string from the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://developer.adobe.com/developer-distribution/creative-cloud/docs/guides/plugin-id#starting-from-adobe-developer-distribution"
      }}>{`Developer Distribution portal`}</a>{` while creating a new UXP plugin listing, and using that in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` file. It is particularly important that the IDs in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` and Developer Distribution portal match, or the plugin will not pass the validation step.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert slots="text, text2" variant="info" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`See `}<a parentName="p" {...{

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "multi-channel-distribution"
    }}>{`Multi-channel distribution`}</h3>
    <p>{`If you plan to distribute your plugin through any channels—either marketplaces or direct distribution—that `}<em parentName="p">{`also include`}</em>{` the Creative Cloud Marketplace, you should `}<strong parentName="p">{`use two different IDs`}</strong>{`: one exclusively for the Creative Cloud Marketplace and one for the other channels.`}</p>
    <p>{`This is particularly important for paid plugins. When a user attempts to install a plugin purchased from a third-party marketplace, the Creative Cloud Desktop application will `}<strong parentName="p">{`validate the plugin's ID against their Adobe ID`}</strong>{`. When the plugin'sID matches only the Marketplace distribution, the installation will be rejected because the purchase cannot be verified.`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">
        <p parentName="li">{`❌ `}<strong parentName="p">{`With the same ID for all distribution channels`}</strong>{` (including the Creative Cloud Marketplace):`}</p>
        <ol parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ol">{`A user purchases a UXP plugin from a third-party marketplace.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ol">{`The Creative Cloud Desktop (CCD) application is in charge of the installation process and checks whether the plugin is available in the Creative Cloud Marketplace.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ol">{`If the plugin is indeed listed in Adobe's Marketplace, the CCD application will check whether the current user (their Adobe ID) has purchased the plugin there.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ol">{`Since the purchase hasn't happened on Adobe's Marketplace, the user isn't entitled to access the plugin—hence the installation fails.`}</li>
        </ol>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul">
        <p parentName="li">{`✅ `}<strong parentName="p">{`With a different ID for the Creative Cloud Marketplace`}</strong>{`:`}</p>
        <ol parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ol">{`A user purchases a UXP plugin from a third-party marketplace.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ol">{`The Creative Cloud Desktop (CCD) application is in charge of the installation process and checks whether the plugin is available in the Creative Cloud Marketplace.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ol">{`The plugin is not available in the Creative Cloud Marketplace; no user's entitlement check is performed, and the plugin can be successfully installed.`}</li>
        </ol>
      </li>
    </ul>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "host-applications"
    }}>{`Host Applications`}</h3>
    <p>{`UXP plugin `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.ccx`}</inlineCode>{` installers can target only one host application at a time; in fact, the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../plugins/concepts/manifest/index.md#host"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`host`}</inlineCode></a>{` property in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` file must be a single object of type `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../plugins/concepts/manifest/index.md#hostdefinition"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`HostDefinition`}</inlineCode></a>{`.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Only during development`}</strong>{`, for convenience, you can assign to the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`host`}</inlineCode>{` property an array of `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`HostDefinition`}</inlineCode>{` objects, allowing the plugin to be loaded in multiple applications simultaneously.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`// host as one HostDefinition object
// ✅ can be used in production
"host": {
  "app": "premierepro", "minVersion": "25.6.0"
}

// host as an array of HostDefinition objects
// ⚠️ only for development! ⚠️
"host": [
  { "app": "premierepro", "minVersion": "25.6.0" },
  { "app": "ps", "minVersion": "25.0.0" }
]
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert slots="text" variant="info" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`If an array is present, the `}<strong parentName="p">{`UXP Developer Tool will automatically package the plugin for the first host application`}</strong>{`, by converting the array into a single `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`HostDefinition`}</inlineCode>{` object under the hood.`}</p>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
;
MDXContent.isMDXComponent = true;
